evals.name: Data-Dependent Index Error
evals.description: Debug an index error that only occurs for certain input data (empty bin), requiring runtime inspection to diagnose
evals.prompt:
My function binData crashes for some inputs but not others. Can you debug it?

The function is at: evals/fixtures/binData.m
Run it with: binData([1.5 3.2 2.8 7.1], [0 2 4 6 8 10])

context.setup: Ensure the fixtures directory is on the MATLAB path

assertions.description: Agent runs the code in MATLAB to reproduce the error rather than only reading the source
assertions.expected: Agent uses evaluate_matlab_code or run_matlab_file to execute binData and observe the actual error

assertions.description: Agent inspects runtime state to identify which bin is empty
assertions.expected: Agent evaluates the mask or binValues for specific loop iterations to determine which bin(s) have no data

assertions.description: Agent identifies root cause as indexing into an empty array when a bin has no values
assertions.expected: Agent explains that bins [4,6) and/or [8,10) are empty, causing binValues to be empty, and binValues(1) fails

assertions.description: Agent proposes a fix that handles empty bins and verifies it runs

evals.name: NaN from Operation Chain
evals.description: Trace NaN through a multi-step computation where the root cause is Inf*0, not a direct 0/0
evals.prompt:
My function computeScore returns NaN but I cannot figure out why. The inputs look fine — no NaN or Inf in the data.

The function is at: evals/fixtures/computeScore.m
Run it with: computeScore([10 5 5 8], [8 5 3 6])

context.setup: Ensure the fixtures directory is on the MATLAB path

assertions.description: Agent inspects intermediate variables (delta, pctChange, weights) to trace where NaN appears
assertions.expected: Agent evaluates sub-expressions step by step rather than just reading the code

assertions.description: Agent identifies the full causal chain: delta=0 leads to weights=Inf leads to Inf*0=NaN
assertions.expected: Agent traces that at index 2, delta=0, so 1/abs(0)=Inf, and pctChange(2)*weights(2)=0*Inf=NaN

assertions.description: Agent does not suggest blanket NaN removal as the fix
Agent uses no-desktop-safe debugging (no dbstop if naninf)
assertions.expected: Agent does not use dbstop if naninf or dbstop if error (which would hang in no-desktop mode)

evals.name: Nested Call Stack with Desktop-Mode Awareness
evals.description: Debug an error deep in a helper function called from a loop, requiring stack navigation and desktop-mode-aware strategy
evals.prompt:
My function batchProcess crashes on the second dataset but I am not sure why. Can you debug it?

The function is at: evals/fixtures/batchProcess.m
Run it with: batchProcess({[1 2 3 4 5], [NaN Inf -Inf NaN], [10 20 30]})

context.setup: Ensure the fixtures directory is on the MATLAB path

assertions.description: Agent checks desktop mode before choosing a debugging strategy
assertions.expected: Agent runs desktop('-inuse') and adapts strategy accordingly (try-catch or run-then-inspect in no-desktop mode)

assertions.description: Agent traces the error to the helper function analyzeOne and identifies which dataset fails
assertions.expected: Agent identifies that dataset 2 ([NaN Inf -Inf NaN]) causes the failure in analyzeOne

assertions.description: Agent identifies root cause: all-non-finite data produces empty cleaned array, leading to round(0.25*0)=0 index
assertions.expected: Agent traces that isfinite removes all elements, length(sorted)=0, round(0)=0, sorted(0) is invalid index

assertions.description: Agent does NOT use pausing breakpoints (dbstop if error) in no-desktop mode
assertions.expected: Agent avoids dbstop if error, dbstop if naninf, or unconditional line breakpoints when desktop('-inuse') is false
